rationale: Every code claim pins to this tree. internal/panel is config-clean today (go list -deps: 0 internal/config), ApproveThreshold() is the single home at panel.go:76, and the new optional approve_threshold field matches the existing json:",omitempty" style (Abandoned/AbandonReason); a legacy panel.json omitting it stays byte-identical N-1. PanelGateDecision(f GateFacts) Decision is already a pure, config-free function, so R8's 'add no config input' is consistent, and ReviewerCountNote(int,int) stays leaf-safe. gate.go:257 already carries a threshold>0 guard reinforcing R5/R7's no-always-pass floor. cmd/mindspec is cobra: a config parent + show subcommand via rootCmd.AddCommand exactly mirrors otelCmd/setup/status, and renderConfig mirrors otel's testable pure render. Spec 108's In Scope (trace/approve/validate/contextpack/doctor/.golangci.yml/OWNERSHIP/tests, cmd marked unchanged) never touches internal/config, internal/panel, or cmd source — the non-collision claim holds. One advisory pin below; it falsifies no requirement or acceptance criterion.
findings: ADR-0035 touchpoint (advisory, plan-time): internal/guard imports internal/config (internal/guard/guard.go:9), so internal/config CANNOT import internal/guard — a cycle. The spec's 'a config validation error is a guard-style failure' must therefore be honored by config formatting the recovery line at the message level (or the caller wrapping in guard.NewFailure), mirroring internal/panel — itself a leaf that emits plain Block text while the caller supplies the guard/exit-code contract. Acceptance test TestLoad_RefusesUnweakenableKnobs only asserts Load returns an error, so nothing is falsified; a one-line note in the spec (or plan) would keep an impl from dead-ending on `guard.NewFailure` inside config.

Non-blocking observation: the current config.Load callers (complete.go, next.go recoveryConfig, impl.go, release.go) swallow Load errors and fall back to DefaultConfig, so R5's refusals surface to a human only through the new `mindspec config show` (R9, in scope) and any future strict path — consistent with the spec (Load refuses; the config-free gate never consumes config per R8), just worth noting the refusal's reach is narrower than the prose might imply until 110+ enforcement.

Confirmed consistent: R7's record-side [1,ExpectedReviewers] bound vs R5's config-side [1,sum(reviewers.count)] bound is a deliberate asymmetry (substituted-quorum divergence), not a contradiction; yaml.v3 unmarshal into a DefaultConfig()-populated struct gives the backfill mechanism the Enforcement.PanelGate comment already relies on, so absent panel:/runner:/loop: keys round-trip to defaults.
